first-program.label: Your First Python Program
first-program.summary: Write and run your first Python program: Hello, World!

q1.title: Write Your First Program
q1.prompt: Create a Python script that prints 'Hello, World!'

help.concept: The print function in Python outputs text to the console.
help.hint_1: Remember to use parentheses with the print function.
help.hint_2: Make sure to include quotes around the text.

q2.title: Modify Your Program
q2.prompt: Change your program to print 'Hello, [Your Name]!'
q2.hint: You can replace 'World' with your name.

help.concept: You can customize the output of the print function by changing the string inside it.
help.hint_1: Make sure to keep the print function intact.
help.hint_2: Use your actual name in the string.

q3.title: Add a New Line
q3.prompt: Modify your program to print 'Hello, [Your Name]!' on one line and 'Welcome to Python!' on the next line.
q3.hint: Focus on the programming task being asked for, not on copying final solution text.

help.concept: Build the solution from the behavior the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which steps, functions, or statements are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the solution based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

q4.title: Identify the Output
q4.prompt: What will the following code output? `print('Hello, World!')` The output will be: `Hello, ____!`
q4.hint: Think about what the print function does.

help.concept: The print function outputs the string exactly as it is written.
help.hint_1: Focus on the text inside the quotes.
help.hint_2: Remember that 'the missing term' is part of the output.

q5.title: Understanding Print Function
q5.prompt: What does the print function do in Python?
q5.hint: Focus on the main concept being tested and eliminate choices that describe something different.

help.concept: Choose the option that matches the core idea described in the question without relying on repeated wording from the answer choices.
help.hint_1: Rule out options that describe a different role, behavior, or concept than the question is testing.
help.hint_2: Pick the option that best matches the concept, not the one that simply repeats familiar wording.

options.b: It outputs text to the console.
options.c: It reads input from the user.

1.title: Introduction to Python
1.bodyMarkdown: In this lesson, you will learn how to write and execute your first Python program, which will print 'Hello, World!' to the console.
